body:
Nevermind, I managed to achieve this with custom commands

```yaml
format = """
$username\
$hostname\
$directory\
$git_branch\
${custom.git_status}\
$all\
$line_break\
$sudo\
$shell\
$character"""

[git_status]
# Configure, but disable git status because we will manually call it in
# custom.git_status
disabled = true
format = '[\[$conflicted$ahead_behind$staged$modified$untracked\]]($style)'

conflicted = '=${count} '
ahead = '⇡${count} '
behind = '⇣${count} '
diverged = '⇕${count} '
untracked = '?${count} '
stashed = '$${count} '
modified = '!${count} '
staged = '+${count} '
renamed = '»${count} '
deleted = '✘${count} '

[custom.git_status]
style = "magenta"
require_repo = true
when = true

# Strip away colors, trim string and wrap in `[ ]`
command = """
starship module git_status | sed -E 's/\\x1B\\[[0-9;]*[mK]//g' | xargs | sed 's/^\\(.*\\)$/[\\1]/'
"""
format = '[$output]($style)'
```
